整套首页
unit_11 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_11

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 394 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC SINGLE
As a history, Bunker's book can seem ____________, prone as it is to diverting asides such as an engaging disquisition on the political science of early modern Europe.As a history, Bunker's book can seem ____________, prone as it is to diverting asides such as an engaging disquisition on the political science of early modern Europe.
答案：E　haphazard
engageengagingprone
engage: standard unit headword | engaging: standard unit headword | prone: standard unit headword
#2 Sentence Equivalence
There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the ______ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the ______ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.
答案：C, D　novel; original
originalprolongprotracted
original: standard unit headword | prolong: standard unit headword | protracted: standard unit headword
#3 Text Completion
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
renaissanceperplexprecede
renaissance: standard unit headword | perplex: standard unit headword | precede: standard unit headword
#4 TC DOUBLE
To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　question the adequacy; conducive to
emergenceterrain
emergence: standard unit headword | terrain: standard unit headword
#5 TC TRIPLE
Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded
precipitateinterest
precipitate: standard unit headword | interest: standard unit headword
#6 Text Completion
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill
paucityvirtuallyproliferation
paucity: standard unit headword | virtually: standard unit headword | proliferation: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　independent; freewheeling
sociablefinicky

答案：B, F　sanctioning; endorsing

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　slowdown; gloomy; temporary

Blank 2

#16 Text Completion

With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　innate; harmonizes with; adapt
intrudecircuitry
intrude: standard unit headword | circuitry: standard unit headword

It arouses the same (iii)_______ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror

That guild of experts has always appraised the economic stimulation plan as bootless, while the advocates of the policy do not take their ______ evaluation for granted.That guild of experts has always appraised the economic stimulation plan as bootless, while the advocates of the policy do not take their ______ evaluation for granted.
答案：C, F　detracting; deprecatory
bootless
bootless: standard unit headword
#51 Text Completion
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
purposeful
purposeful: standard unit headword

While acknowledging behaviors the Prime Minister took in order to remain in office were (i)______, some politicians nevertheless believed that this small amount of (ii)______ was justified to keep a reforming government in office.While acknowledging behaviors the Prime Minister took in order to remain in office were (i)______, some politicians nevertheless believed that this small amount of (ii)______ was justified to keep a reforming government in office.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　unethical; skullduggery
skullduggery
skullduggery: standard unit headword

The tribes’ ______ a settled mode of living was derived from their long-standing traditions, which, though differing from one tribal group to another, always included a resistance to nomadic lifestyles.The tribes’ ______ a settled mode of living was derived from their long-standing traditions, which, though differing from one tribal group to another, always included a resistance to nomadic lifestyles.
答案：B, C　proclivity toward; predilection for

nomadic: standard unit headword
#54 Sentence Equivalence
There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is ______ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.There is a revelation on almost every page of this book, and the author’s prose is ______ in the best possible way: blunt, sweet, off-kilter, and often quite funny.
答案：D, F　awkward; ungainly
off-kilter

#55 Text Completion
Instead of demonstrating the ______ of archaeological applications of electronic remote sensing, the pioneering study became, to some skeptics, an illustration of the imprudence of interpreting sites based on virtual archeology.Instead of demonstrating the ______ of archaeological applications of electronic remote sensing, the pioneering study became, to some skeptics, an illustration of the imprudence of interpreting sites based on virtual archeology.
答案：E　promise
virtual
virtual: standard unit headword
#56 Text Completion
Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)______, the most (ii)______ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii) ______. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)______, the most (ii)______ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii) ______. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　detectable; violent; minuscule
effectminuscule
effect: standard unit headword | minuscule: standard unit headword

In politics, tactical calculations about which groups a candidate should appeal to are never pleasant, but they are not always ______, and sometimes they are necessary.In politics, tactical calculations about which groups a candidate should appeal to are never pleasant, but they are not always ______, and sometimes they are necessary.
答案：B, D　sleazy; sordid
tactical
tactical: standard unit headword

Despite her rather ______ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.Despite her rather ______ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.
答案：A, C　unconventional; quirky
flamboyant
flamboyant: standard unit headword

答案：B　effecting

There were few (ii)______, however, so the form (iii)______.

Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in

However, water mill’s great capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)______ the money required to build the mill.

The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring

The (i) _________ of Harvard climatologist Robert DeCourcy Ward was that faculty attached to related science departments would take it upon themselves to learn enough meteorology to prepare and teach an elementary course. With their interest sufficiently (ii) __________ , students might (iii) _________ more advanced courses, thus opening the door for the establishment of meteorology curricula in colleges across the country.In the early twentieth century, there were so few meteorologists on college faculties that finding instructors was difficult. The (i) _________ of Harvard climatologist Robert DeCourcy Ward was that faculty attached to related science departments would take it upon themselves to learn enough meteorology to prepare and teach an elementary course. Blank 2

Vallabhbhai Patel, Subhas Chandra Bose, and Rabindranath Tagore have all benefited from (i) interprstations of each man's life and thought. The artists sought to (i) _________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) ________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) _________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.The artists sought to (i) _________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) ________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) _________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　affirm; marginalized; unsettle
cultivate
cultivate: standard unit headword
#102 TC SINGLE
The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.
答案：A　perplexing
perplex
perplex: standard unit headword
#103 SE
Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal ____________ per se.Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal ____________ per se.
答案：A, F　complexity; intricacy
interest
interest: standard unit headword

Ultimately, the young idealists (i)__________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii)_________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii)____________ .Ultimately, the young idealists (i)__________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii)_________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii)____________ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　did a great disservice to; nullified; begotten

effect: standard unit headword

Influenza is __________ virus: it is full of surprises and newness, prone to mutation and reassortment.Influenza is __________ virus: it is full of surprises and newness, prone to mutation and reassortment.
答案：E　a protean
prone
prone: standard unit headword
#106 SE
Although high-purity metals are typically too __________ for practical use, small amounts of impurities in certain commercially pure metals impart adequate strength for some structural applications.Although high-purity metals are typically too __________ for practical use, small amounts of impurities in certain commercially pure metals impart adequate strength for some structural applications.
答案：D, F　soft; malleable
malleable
malleable: standard unit headword

It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i)_______ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii)_______, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i)_______ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii)_______, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　depleted; overharvested
proliferation
proliferation: standard unit headword

Some history books are pathbreaking. (i) ___________ conventional wisdom, they inspire scholars to (ii) ____________ lines of inquiry virtually unimagined before their publication. (i) ___________ conventional wisdom, they inspire scholars to (ii) ____________ lines of inquiry virtually unimagined before their publication. Other works, valuable in their own way, fill in gaps by challenging readers to rethink issues that had been thought to be (iii) _____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　Revising; pursue; resolved

答案：A　nuance
nuance

答案：B, F　collective; social

emerge: standard unit headword

Blank 1

答案：C, E　impede; cerebral

But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (ii) by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as(i) as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. 答案：C, E　obscure; opaque

Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder

答案：C　prudent

Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.
Blank 1

答案：A　linear

答案：D, F　paucity; dearth

答案：B, E　contemplative; ruminative

#154 TC TRIPLE
Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　invariably face opposition; accustomed; militate against
original
original: standard unit headword

The company CEO' s easygoing manner (i) his(ii) the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.The company CEO' s easygoing manner (i) his(ii) the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　belied; intensity on

interest: standard unit headword

As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)______ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)______ leader.The (i)______ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)______ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)______ leader.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：D　dearth
proliferation

答案：E, F　anonymous; obscure
cultivate
cultivate: standard unit headword

Among chess aficionados, there have been many doomsday scenarios about people (i)________ the game as a result of the superiority of computer chess programs over human players. Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii)________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii)________ of human chess.Among chess aficionados, there have been many doomsday scenarios about people (i)________ the game as a result of the superiority of computer chess programs over human players. Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii)________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii)________ of human chess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　losing interest in; spurious; quality

答案：A, E　elucidation; demystification

For instance, he deplores the Royal Shakespeare Company's eight-and-a-half-hour version of Nicholas Nickel by, which many found (ii) .

答案：A　volatile
cunning
cunning: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　obviate; displace

答案：C, E　speculation; conjecture

Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious

Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Blank 2
Blank 3

During the Renaissance, the use of optical lenses, which were capable of projecting images onto blank canvases, greatly aided artists by allowing them to accurately observe and depict the external world; in other words, these lenses were instrumental in conveying ______.During the Renaissance, the use of optical lenses, which were capable of projecting images onto blank canvases, greatly aided artists by allowing them to accurately observe and depict the external world; in other words, these lenses were instrumental in conveying ______.
答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

答案：C, D　sterile; unfruitful

#205 Text Completion
The difficulty for nineteenth-century advocates of the claim that forests helped regulate climate was that their argument (i)______ historical anecdote and observations. Proving the forest-climate link through verifiable and experimental scientific means rather than observation was (ii)______ for these individuals, a situation that eventually led to the link (iii)______ justifications for forest conservation.The difficulty for nineteenth-century advocates of the claim that forests helped regulate climate was that their argument (i)______ historical anecdote and observations. Proving the forest-climate link through verifiable and experimental scientific means rather than observation was (ii)______ for these individuals, a situation that eventually led to the link (iii)______ justifications for forest conservation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　employed; problematic; dropping out of
observationanecdote
observation: standard unit headword | anecdote: standard unit headword
#206 Sentence Equivalence
Although the essayist’s arguments did not ______ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.Although the essayist’s arguments did not ______ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.
答案：B, E　confound; perplex
perplex
perplex: standard unit headword

The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are :while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are :while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.
答案：A, B　overblown; inflated

The claim that these differences are (ii)______, or that deep down, everybody acts only to further their own interests, (iii)______ observations and deep-seated human practices of moral evaluation.Common sense tells us some people are more (i)______ than others. The claim that these differences are (ii)______, or that deep down, everybody acts only to further their own interests, (iii)______ observations and deep-seated human practices of moral evaluation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　altruistic; illusory; contradicts

Braque and Picasso created Cubism together, but the mercurial Picasso is regarded as the star of the movement while Braque, because he was so much more (ii)______ , has been relatively (iii)______ by history.The quiet, careful member of a collaborative, creative pair may naturally be (i)______ by posterity in comparison with the brash, dramatic one. Blank 3

答案：A, C　sparse; paltry

答案：C, E　paltry; meager

Blank 2
Blank 3

#220 SE
Dogs' intake of olfactory information is less ____________ than that of humans because dogs exhale through the side slits of their nostrils, keeping a continuous flow of inhaled air in their snouts for smelling.Dogs' intake of olfactory information is less ____________ than that of humans because dogs exhale through the side slits of their nostrils, keeping a continuous flow of inhaled air in their snouts for smelling.
答案：C, F　punctuated; intermittent

答案：A, E　dangerous; perilous

Blank 2

They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .

答案：D, F　prevented; precluded

答案：A, C　nuanced; complex
nuance

答案：C　specious

答案：A, C　hackneyed; pedestrian

答案：B　obscure
interest

答案：B, F　inaccessible; opaque

It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. 答案：C, E　minimal; trifling

It must have been a (i)______ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)______.It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)______ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely

#264 Sentence Equivalence
Although one can adduce myriad examples of ecosystem disruption by non-indigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survive in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystems they have invaded.Although one can adduce myriad examples of ecosystem disruption by non-indigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survive in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystems they have invaded.
答案：A, B　minimal; trifling

effect: standard unit headword
#265 Text Completion
The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood

interest: standard unit headword
#266 Text Completion
Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth—for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes bad been. Given such a (i)______ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)______ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth—for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes bad been. Given such a (i)______ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)______ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.

Blank 2
答案：A, E　cavalier disregard for; ironic

Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii)______.It would be naïve to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer’s true state of mind, since the (i)______ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　penalty; balanced reflection

答案：D, F　capture; secure

Blank 1

答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous

With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)______ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)______ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk

答案：B, F　capricious; impulsive

In truth, the preservation of the reef (iii)______, a combination of active government intervention and the beneficial effects of responsible tourism.(i)______ have often shrilled that Australia’s Great Barrier Reef is dying, a result of agricultural runoff from the (ii)______ Queensland coast. In truth, the preservation of the reef (iii)______, a combination of active government intervention and the beneficial effects of responsible tourism.

Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. Such (ii)______ feed plus other environmental side effects undermine the expected (iii)______ fish farming.Overall, fish farming may provide a (i)______ wild fish stocks, but for certain species, the farmers cause a net loss of wild fish. Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. #289 Text Completion
The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)______ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)______ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)______ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)______ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)______ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)______ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristic

They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)______ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animals energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)______ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)______ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　onset; put up with constant hunger; mimic

Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______ .Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)______ . In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating

Many creative photographers were delighted to find in instant photography a mode that encouraged them to stop viewing photography as ______ and start viewing it as something they could handle with spontaneity, even derision.Many creative photographers were delighted to find in instant photography a mode that encouraged them to stop viewing photography as ______ and start viewing it as something they could handle with spontaneity, even derision.
答案：A　sacrosanct

Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)______. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)______ her attempt to (iii)______ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)______. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)______ her attempt to (iii)______ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain

After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii)______ , having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has setin.Acertain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i)______ . This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.A transformative scientific idea that emerged in the eighteenth century was the realization that slow, inexorable geological processes follow the basic laws of physics and chemistry. This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.

答案：A, D, I　an obvious; followed; revolutionary for

#392 Text Completion
Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive

#394 Text Completion
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique
